sectiondef.header: LwM2M Objects managed by OMA for LwM2M tech specification.

description.para: Objects in this range have IDs from 0 to 1023.

sectiondef.header: LwM2M Objects produced by 3rd party Standards Development

description.para: Refer to the OMA LightweightM2M (LwM2M) Object and Resource Registry: http://www.openmobilealliance.org/wp/OMNA/LwM2M/LwM2MRegistry.html

sectiondef.header: Power source types used for the "Available Power Sources" resource of

briefdescription.para: Max value for Available Power Source type.

sectiondef.header: Error codes used for the "Error Code" resource of the LwM2M Device

description.para: An LwM2M client can register one of the following error codes via the lwm2m_device_add_err() function.

sectiondef.header: Battery status codes used for the "Battery Status" resource (3/0/20)

description.para: As the battery status changes, an LwM2M client can set one of the following codes via: lwm2m_set_u8("3/0/20", [battery status])

briefdescription.para: The battery is operating normally and not on power.
The battery is fully charged and the charger is still connected.
The battery is low on charge.
The battery information is not available.

description.para: An LwM2M client or the LwM2M Firmware Update object use the following codes to represent the LwM2M Firmware Update state (5/0/3).

detaileddescription.para: Before downloading or after successful updating.
The data sequence is being downloaded.
The whole data sequence has been downloaded.
The device is being updated.

sectiondef.header: LWM2M Firmware Update object result codes

description.para: After processing a firmware update, the client sets the result via one of the following codes via lwm2m_set_u8("5/0/5", [result code])

briefdescription.para: Not enough flash memory for the new firmware package.
Out of RAM during downloading process.
Integrity check failure for new downloaded package.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M client events are passed back to the event_cb function in lwm2m_rd_client_start()

briefdescription.para: One response is expected for the next message.
Next message is the last one.
Different traffic states of the LwM2M socket.

detaileddescription.para: This information can be used to give hints for the network interface that can decide what kind of power management should be used.
These hints are given from CoAP layer messages, so usage of DTLS might affect the actual number of expected datagrams.
LwM2M send status are generated back to the lwm2m_send_cb_t function in lwm2m_send_cb()

briefdescription.para: Security modes as defined in LwM2M Security object.
Callback function called when a socket error is encountered.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* lwm2m_observe_cb_t) (enum lwm2m_observe_event event, struct lwm2m_obj_path *path, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: lwm2m_observe_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Observe callback indicating observer adds and deletes, and notification ACKs and timeouts.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to user_data buffer, as provided in send_traceable_notification(). Used to determine for which data the ACKed/timed out notification was.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* lwm2m_ctx_event_cb_t) (struct lwm2m_ctx *ctx, enum lwm2m_rd_client_event event)
memberdef.name: lwm2m_ctx_event_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Callback type for filtering cached time series samples.

detaileddescription.para: Returning false skips storing the provided sample in the LwM2M cache.

parameterdescription.para: Object path of the cached resource.
Sample produced by the engine for the cache.

simplesect.para: true to keep the sample, false to discard it.

memberdef.definition: typedef void *(* lwm2m_engine_get_data_cb_t) (uint16_t obj_inst_id, uint16_t res_id, uint16_t res_inst_id, size_t *data_len)
memberdef.argsstring: )(uint16_t obj_inst_id, uint16_t res_id, uint16_t res_inst_id, size_t *data_len)
memberdef.name: lwm2m_engine_get_data_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Asynchronous callback to get a resource buffer and length.

detaileddescription.para: Prior to accessing the data buffer of a resource, the engine can use this callback to get the buffer pointer and length instead of using the resource's data buffer.
The client or LwM2M objects can register a function of this type via: lwm2m_register_read_callback() lwm2m_register_pre_write_callback()

parameterdescription.para: Object instance ID generating the callback.
Resource instance ID generating the callback (typically 0 for non-multi instance resources).

simplesect.para: Callback returns a pointer to the data buffer or NULL for failure.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* lwm2m_engine_set_data_cb_t) (uint16_t obj_inst_id, uint16_t res_id, uint16_t res_inst_id, uint8_t *data, uint16_t data_len, bool last_block, size_t total_size, size_t offset)
memberdef.argsstring: )(uint16_t obj_inst_id, uint16_t res_id, uint16_t res_inst_id, uint8_t *data, uint16_t data_len, bool last_block, size_t total_size, size_t offset)
memberdef.name: lwm2m_engine_set_data_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Asynchronous callback when data has been set to a resource buffer.

detaileddescription.para: After changing the data of a resource buffer, the LwM2M engine can make use of this callback to pass the data back to the client or LwM2M objects.

On a block-wise transfers the handler is called multiple times with the data blocks and increasing offset. The last block has the last_block flag set to true. Beginning of the block transfer has the offset set to 0.
A function of this type can be registered via: lwm2m_register_validate_callback() lwm2m_register_post_write_callback()

parameterdescription.para: Object instance ID generating the callback.
Resource instance ID generating the callback (typically 0 for non-multi instance resources).
Flag used during block transfer to indicate the last block of data. For non-block transfers this is always false.
Expected total size of data for a block transfer. For non-block transfers this is 0.
Offset of the data block. For non-block transfers this is always 0.

simplesect.para: Callback returns a negative error code (errno.h) indicating reason of failure or 0 for success.

detaileddescription.para: Various object instance and resource-based events in the LwM2M engine can trigger a callback of this function type: object instance create, and object instance delete.
Register a function of this type via: lwm2m_register_create_callback() lwm2m_register_delete_callback()

parameterdescription.para: Object instance ID generating the callback.

simplesect.para: Callback returns a negative error code (errno.h) indicating reason of failure or 0 for success.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* lwm2m_engine_execute_cb_t) (uint16_t obj_inst_id, uint8_t *args, uint16_t args_len)
memberdef.name: lwm2m_engine_execute_cb_t

detaileddescription.para: Resource executes trigger a callback of this type.
Register a function of this type via: lwm2m_register_exec_callback()

parameterdescription.para: Object instance ID generating the callback.
Pointer to execute arguments payload. (This can be NULL if no arguments are provided)
Length of argument payload in bytes.

simplesect.para: Callback returns a negative error code (errno.h) indicating reason of failure or 0 for success.

briefdescription.para: Register a new error code with LwM2M Device object.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Set data callback for firmware block transfer.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients use this function to register a callback for receiving the block transfer data when performing a firmware update.

parameterdescription.para: A callback function to receive the block transfer data

briefdescription.para: Get the data callback for firmware block transfer writes.

simplesect.para: A registered callback function to receive the block transfer data

briefdescription.para: Set data callback for firmware block transfer.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients use this function to register a callback for receiving the block transfer data when performing a firmware update.

parameterdescription.para: A callback function to receive the block transfer data

briefdescription.para: Get the data callback for firmware block transfer writes.

simplesect.para: A registered callback function to receive the block transfer data

briefdescription.para: Set callback for firmware update cancel.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients use this function to register a callback to perform actions on firmware update cancel.

parameterdescription.para: A callback function perform actions on firmware update cancel.

briefdescription.para: Get a callback for firmware update cancel.

simplesect.para: A registered callback function perform actions on firmware update cancel.

briefdescription.para: Set data callback for firmware update cancel.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients use this function to register a callback to perform actions on firmware update cancel.

parameterdescription.para: A callback function perform actions on firmware update cancel.

briefdescription.para: Get the callback for firmware update cancel.

simplesect.para: A registered callback function perform actions on firmware update cancel.

briefdescription.para: Set data callback to handle firmware update execute events.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients use this function to register a callback for receiving the update resource "execute" operation on the LwM2M Firmware Update object.

parameterdescription.para: A callback function to receive the execute event.

briefdescription.para: Get the event callback for firmware update execute events.

simplesect.para: A registered callback function to receive the execute event.

briefdescription.para: Set data callback to handle firmware update execute events.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients use this function to register a callback for receiving the update resource "execute" operation on the LwM2M Firmware Update object.

parameterdescription.para: A callback function to receive the execute event.

briefdescription.para: Get the event callback for firmware update execute events.

simplesect.para: A registered callback function to receive the execute event.

briefdescription.para: Set callback to handle software activation requests.

detaileddescription.para: The callback will be executed when the LWM2M execute operation gets called on the corresponding object's Activate resource instance.

parameterdescription.para: The instance number to set the callback for.
A callback function to receive the execute event.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, otherwise a negative integer.

briefdescription.para: Set callback to handle software deactivation requests.

detaileddescription.para: The callback will be executed when the LWM2M execute operation gets called on the corresponding object's Deactivate resource instance.

parameterdescription.para: The instance number to set the callback for.
A callback function to receive the execute event.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, otherwise a negative integer.

briefdescription.para: Set callback to handle software install requests.

detaileddescription.para: The callback will be executed when the LWM2M execute operation gets called on the corresponding object's Install resource instance.

parameterdescription.para: The instance number to set the callback for.
A callback function to receive the execute event.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, otherwise a negative integer.

briefdescription.para: Set callback to handle software uninstall requests.

detaileddescription.para: The callback will be executed when the LWM2M execute operation gets called on the corresponding object's Uninstall resource instance.

parameterdescription.para: The instance number to set the callback for.
A callback function for handling the execute event.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, otherwise a negative integer.

briefdescription.para: Set callback to read software package.

detaileddescription.para: The callback will be executed when the LWM2M read operation gets called on the corresponding object.

parameterdescription.para: The instance number to set the callback for.
A callback function for handling the read event.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, otherwise a negative integer.

briefdescription.para: Set data callback for software management block transfer.

detaileddescription.para: The callback will be executed when the LWM2M block write operation gets called on the corresponding object's resource instance.

parameterdescription.para: The instance number to set the callback for.
A callback function for handling the block write event.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, otherwise a negative integer.

briefdescription.para: Function to be called when a Software Management object instance completed the Install operation.

detaileddescription.para:
obj_inst_id
The Software Management object instance
error_code
The result code of the operation. Zero on success otherwise it should be a negative integer.
return 0 on success, otherwise a negative integer.

briefdescription.para: Set callback to read log data.

detaileddescription.para: The callback will be executed when the LWM2M read operation gets called on the corresponding object.

parameterdescription.para: A callback function for handling the read event.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct lwm2m_ctx *client_ctx, const struct lwm2m_obj_path *path, uint32_t period_s)
memberdef.name: lwm2m_update_observer_min_period

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients use this function to modify the pmin attribute for an observation being made. Example to update the pmin of a temperature sensor value being observed: lwm2m_update_observer_min_period(client_ctx, &LWM2M_OBJ(3303, 0, 5700), 5);

parameterdescription.para: Value of pmin to be given (in seconds).

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct lwm2m_ctx *client_ctx, const struct lwm2m_obj_path *path, uint32_t period_s)
memberdef.name: lwm2m_update_observer_max_period

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients use this function to modify the pmax attribute for an observation being made. Example to update the pmax of a temperature sensor value being observed: lwm2m__update_observer_max_period(client_ctx, &LWM2M_OBJ(3303, 0, 5700), 5);

parameterdescription.para: Value of pmax to be given (in seconds).

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients use this function to create non-default LwM2M objects: Example to create first temperature sensor object: lwm2m_create_obj_inst(&LWM2M_OBJ(3303, 0));

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients use this function to delete LwM2M objects.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Locks the registry for this thread.

detaileddescription.para: Use this function before writing to multiple resources. This halts the lwm2m main thread until all the write-operations are finished.

briefdescription.para: Unlocks the registry previously locked by lwm2m_registry_lock().

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct lwm2m_obj_path *path, const char *data_ptr, uint16_t data_len)
memberdef.name: lwm2m_set_opaque

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.
0 for success or negative in case of error.
0 for success or negative in case of error.
0 for success or negative in case of error.
0 for success or negative in case of error.
0 for success or negative in case of error.
0 for success or negative in case of error.
0 for success or negative in case of error.
0 for success or negative in case of error.
0 for success or negative in case of error.
0 for success or negative in case of error.
0 for success or negative in case of error.
0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: NOTE: Value type must match the target resource as this function does not do any type conversion. See struct lwm2m_res_item for list of resource types.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

parameterdescription.para: Data buffer to copy data into

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

parameterdescription.para: String buffer to copy data into

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

parameterdescription.para: u8 buffer to copy data into

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

parameterdescription.para: u16 buffer to copy data into

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

parameterdescription.para: u32 buffer to copy data into

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

parameterdescription.para: s8 buffer to copy data into

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

parameterdescription.para: s16 buffer to copy data into

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

parameterdescription.para: s32 buffer to copy data into

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

parameterdescription.para: s64 buffer to copy data into

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

parameterdescription.para: bool buffer to copy data into

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

parameterdescription.para: double buffer to copy data into

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

parameterdescription.para: lwm2m_objlnk buffer to copy data into

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.
0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients can use this to set the callback function for resource reads when data handling in the LwM2M engine needs to be bypassed. For example reading back opaque binary data from external storage.
This callback should not generally be used for any data that might be observed as engine does not have any knowledge of data changes.
When separate buffer for data should be used, use lwm2m_set_res_buf() instead to set the storage.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: This callback is triggered before setting the value of a resource. It can pass a special data buffer to the engine so that the actual resource value can be calculated later, etc.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: This callback is triggered before setting the value of a resource to the resource data buffer.
The callback allows an LwM2M client or object to validate the data before writing and notify an error if the data should be discarded for any reason (by returning a negative error code).

simplesect.para: All resources that have a validation callback registered are initially decoded into a temporary validation buffer. Make sure that CONFIG_LWM2M_ENGINE_VALIDATION_BUFFER_SIZE is large enough to store each of the validated resources (individually).
0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: This callback is triggered after setting the value of a resource to the resource data buffer.
It allows an LwM2M client or object to post-process the value of a resource or trigger other related resource calculations.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: This event is triggered when the execute method of a resource is enabled.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Set object instance create event callback.

detaileddescription.para: This event is triggered when an object instance is created.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Set object instance delete event callback.

detaileddescription.para: This event is triggered when an object instance is deleted.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct lwm2m_obj_path *path, void *buffer_ptr, uint16_t buffer_len, uint16_t data_len, uint8_t data_flags)
memberdef.name: lwm2m_set_res_buf

briefdescription.para: Set data buffer for a resource.

detaileddescription.para: Use this function to set the data buffer and flags for the specified LwM2M resource.

parameterdescription.para: Length of existing data in the buffer
Data buffer flags (such as read-only, etc)

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Update data size for a resource.

detaileddescription.para: Use this function to set the new size of data in the buffer if you write to a buffer received by lwm2m_get_res_buf().

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct lwm2m_obj_path *path, void **buffer_ptr, uint16_t *buffer_len, uint16_t *data_len, uint8_t *data_flags)
memberdef.name: lwm2m_get_res_buf

briefdescription.para: Get data buffer for a resource.

detaileddescription.para: Use this function to get the data buffer information for the specified LwM2M resource.
If you directly write into the buffer, you must use lwm2m_set_res_data_len() function to update the new size of the written data.
All parameters, except for the pathstr, can be NULL if you don't want to read those values.

parameterdescription.para: Length of existing data in the buffer
Data buffer flags (such as read-only, etc)

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients use this function to create multi-resource instances: Example to create 0 instance of device available power sources: lwm2m_create_res_inst(&LWM2M_OBJ(3, 0, 6, 0));

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: Use this function to remove an existing resource instance

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Update the period of the device service.

detaileddescription.para: Change the duration of the periodic device service that notifies the current time.

parameterdescription.para: New period for the device service (in milliseconds)

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Check whether a path is observed.

parameterdescription.para: LwM2M path as a struct to check

simplesect.para: true when there exists an observation of the same level or lower as the given path, false if it doesn't or path is not a valid LwM2M-path. E.g. true if path refers to a resource and the parent object has an observation, false for the inverse.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients normally do not need to call this function as it is called within lwm2m_rd_client. However, if the client does not use the RD client implementation, it will need to be called manually.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M clients normally do not need to call this function as it is called by lwm2m_rd_client_start(). However, if the client does not use the RD client implementation, it will need to be called manually.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Acknowledge the currently processed request with an empty ACK.

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M engine by default sends piggybacked responses for requests. This function allows to send an empty ACK for a request earlier (from the application callback). The LwM2M engine will then send the actual response as a separate CON message after all callbacks are executed.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct lwm2m_ctx *client_ctx, const char *ep_name, uint32_t flags, lwm2m_ctx_event_cb_t event_cb, lwm2m_observe_cb_t observe_cb)
memberdef.name: lwm2m_rd_client_start

briefdescription.para: Start the LwM2M RD (Registration / Discovery) Client.

detaileddescription.para: The RD client sits just above the LwM2M engine and performs the necessary actions to implement the "Registration interface". For more information see Section "Client Registration Interface" of LwM2M Technical Specification.
NOTE: lwm2m_engine_start() is called automatically by this function.

parameterdescription.para: Flags used to configure current LwM2M session.
Observe callback function called when an observer was added or deleted, and when a notification was acked or has timed out

simplesect.para: 0 for success, -EINPROGRESS when client is already running or negative error codes in case of failure.

detaileddescription.para: The RD client sits just above the LwM2M engine and performs the necessary actions to implement the "Registration interface". For more information see Section "Client Registration Interface" of the LwM2M Technical Specification.

parameterdescription.para: True to deregister the client if registered. False to force close the connection.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: Suspend LwM2M engine. Use case could be when network connection is down. LwM2M Engine indicate before it suspend by LWM2M_RD_CLIENT_EVENT_ENGINE_SUSPENDED event.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: Resume suspended LwM2M engine. After successful resume call engine will do full registration or registration update based on suspended time. Event's LWM2M_RD_CLIENT_EVENT_REGISTRATION_COMPLETE or LWM2M_RD_CLIENT_EVENT_REG_UPDATE_COMPLETE indicate that client is connected to server.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Trigger a Registration Update of the LwM2M RD Client.
Helper function to print path objects' contents to log.

parameterdescription.para: The buffer to use for formatting the string

simplesect.para: Resulting formatted path string

memberdef.argsstring: (struct lwm2m_ctx *ctx, const struct lwm2m_obj_path path_list[], uint8_t path_list_size, lwm2m_send_cb_t reply_cb)
memberdef.name: lwm2m_send_cb

detaileddescription.para: LwM2M SEND operation to given path list asynchronously with confirmation callback

parameterdescription.para: Length of path list. Max size is CONFIG_LWM2M_COMPOSITE_PATH_LIST_SIZE
Callback triggered with confirmation state or NULL if not used

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: Returns LwM2Mclient context

simplesect.para: ctx LwM2M context

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct lwm2m_obj_path *path, struct lwm2m_time_series_elem *data_cache, size_t cache_len)
memberdef.name: lwm2m_enable_cache

detaileddescription.para: Enable data cache for a resource.
Application may enable caching of resource data by allocating buffer for LwM2M engine to use. Buffer must be size of struct lwm2m_time_series_elem times cache_len

parameterdescription.para: LwM2M path to resource as a struct

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Register a filtering callback for cached resource samples.

detaileddescription.para: The callback is invoked whenever the LwM2M engine attempts to append a new sample to the resource cache. Returning false prevents the sample from being stored. Passing a NULL callback removes any previously registered filter.

parameterdescription.para: LwM2M path to the cached resource.
Callback used to decide whether samples should be cached.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or a negative errno code in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Return number of free slots in a cached resource.

parameterdescription.para: LwM2M path to the cached resource.

simplesect.para: Number of free slots, or negative errno code in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Read security mode from selected security object instance.

detaileddescription.para: This data is valid only if RD client is running.

simplesect.para: int Positive values are lwm2m_security_mode_e, negative error codes otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Set default socket options for DTLS connections.

detaileddescription.para: The engine calls this when lwm2m_ctx::set_socketoptions is not overwritten. You can call this from the overwritten callback to set extra options after or before defaults.

simplesect.para: 0 for success or negative in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Set the lwm2m_ctx::set_socketoptions callback for the pull context's client.

detaileddescription.para: This allows setting specific socket options on the socket that is used to pull firmware updates. The callback will be called after the pull context socket has been created and before it will connect.

parameterdescription.para: A callback function to set sockopts for the pull context client.

briefdescription.para: Maximum value for Objlnk resource fields.
Run bootstrap procedure in current session.
